**Page**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/edinburgh-v1/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/LearningsPage.tsx` - **Timeline Component**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/edinburgh-v1/apps/web/src/components/learnings/LearningsTimeline.tsx` - **Timeline Item Component**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/edinburgh-v1/apps/web/src/components/learnings/TimelineItem.tsx` - **Trend Chart**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/edinburgh-v1/apps/web/src/components/charts/LearningsTrendChart.tsx` ### 2. **Route & Navigation** - **Route**: `/dashboard/learnings` (defined in `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/edinburgh-v1/apps/web/src/App.tsx` at line 106) - **Navigation**: Added to the main sidebar at `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/edinburgh-v1/apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx` (line 37) with the BookOpen icon - **Display Name**: "Learning Feed" ### 3. **Data Fetching** The `LearningsPage.tsx` fetches data through four main oRPC endpoints: ```typescript // Main learnings list (paginated) orpc.analytics.learnings.list.queryOptions({ input: { days, limit: 100, offset: 0 }, }) // Statistics overview orpc.analytics.learnings.stats.queryOptions({ input: { days } }) // Trend data for charting orpc.analytics.learnings.trend.queryOptions({ input: { days, splitBy }, }) // Available users and projects for filtering orpc.analytics.learnings.users.queryOptions({}) orpc.analytics.learnings.projects.queryOptions({}) ``` **API Handlers** (in `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/edinburgh-v1/apps/api/src/handlers/analytics/learnings.ts`): - `list`: Fetches learnings feed from compound:feedback sessions - `stats`: Gets learnings statistics (total, unique users, unique projects) - `users`: Gets unique users who have used compound:feedback - `projects`: Gets unique projects with learnings - `trend`: Gets learnings trend over time with splits by user_id or repository **API Services** (in `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/edinburgh-v1/apps/api/src/services/learnings.service.ts`): - All learnings data comes from ClickHouse `rudel.session_analytics` table - Data is filtered for sessions containing the `/compound:feedback` slash command - Extracts feedback content from the command arguments within session content ### 4. **Current Empty State** The empty state is implemented in `LearningsTimeline.tsx` (lines 37-56): ```typescript if (learnings.length === 0) { return (

No learnings yet

Learnings will appear here when team members use the{" "} /compound:feedback {" "} command to provide feedback during their coding sessions.

); } ``` ### 5. **Page Layout & Features** The `LearningsPage.tsx` includes: **Header Section**: - Title: "Learning Feed" - Description: "Team feedback and insights from /compound:feedback sessions" - Date picker for filtering by time range **Statistics Overview** (3-column grid): - Total Learnings - Team Members (unique users) - Projects (with learnings) **Filters**: - Multi-select filter for Users - Multi-select filter for Projects - "Clear filters" button **Learnings Trend Chart**: - Displays feedback activity over time - Can split by "Developer" (user_id) or "Repository" - Has cumulative/non-cumulative toggle **Timeline Section**: - Currently shows placeholder text: "Timeline coming soon" (line 218) - Not yet implemented, but has supporting components ready ### 6. **Learning Entry Structure** From the API schema, a `LearningEntry` contains: - `session_id`: Reference to the coding session - `user_id`: Developer who provided feedback - `created_at`: Timestamp of feedback - `type`: "feedback" (hardcoded in service) - `content`: Extracted feedback text from /compound:feedback command - `scope`: "project" (hardcoded in service) - `tags`: Empty array (currently not used) - `project_path`: Which project the feedback was about - `repository`: Optional repository name ### 7. **Timeline Item Component** (When Implemented) The `TimelineItem.tsx` component (ready but not yet used) includes features for: - Type badges with color coding (pattern, antipattern, convention, fix, preference) - Scope badges - Metadata display (user, project, repository) - Tag display - Content truncation at 300 characters with expand/collapse - Link to the original session This is a complete and well-structured learning section with the core infrastructure in place. The main placeholder is the timeline display which is waiting to be connected to the fetched learning data.
